results.title: A malicious user can steal other user's deposits from Vault.sol
results.reasoning: Yes. Audit item #26 describes the exact same issue: in Vault.redeem/withdraw, assets are computed from the full uint256 shares while _burn downcasts shares to uint96, burning too few shares and allowing an attacker with >2^96-1 shares to withdraw disproportionate assets and drain the vault. This matches the reported vulnerability’s root cause (uint96 truncation in Vault._burn), code path (Vault.withdraw -> _withdraw -> _burn), impact (asset theft), and remediation (use SafeCast/wider types or enforce bounds). Fixing the audit’s issue would fix the described vulnerability.

results.title: Vault.mintYieldFee function can be called by anyone to mint Vault Shares to any recipient address
results.reasoning: Yes. Audit item #6 explicitly flags Vault.mintYieldFee as external with no access control and ignoring _yieldFeeRecipient, allowing any caller to mint accrued yield-fee shares to any arbitrary recipient. This is the same function, mechanism, and impact as the described vulnerability. The suggested fix in the description (remove recipient param/use _yieldFeeRecipient) would also remediate the audit’s finding.
